reasoning: The Bulgargaz-Botas agreement has no termination clause, and Bulgarian officials (including the Energy Minister as recently as late May 2025) have confirmed that unilaterally breaking it would cost Bulgaria an estimated BGN 4 billion ($2.2 billion) in penalties. While Bulgargaz has ceased payments and politicians like Boyko Borissov have appealed directly to President Erdogan to cancel the deal, Turkey has strong leverage and seems to be focusing on renegotiation/amendments rather than outright cancellation. The process is progressing slowly, and an official announcement of cancellation by either side before July 1, 2025 (within the next 25 days) is highly unlikely given the massive financial liabilities and the preference for restructuring the contract instead.
